subject: current-affairs
description: General & Presidential Elections 2024 MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: The General Elections of 2024 in Pakistan were the:
mcqs.explanation: The 2024 General Elections were the 12th General Elections in Pakistan's history, following the 11th in 2018 and 10th in 2013.

mcqs.q: The General Elections of 2024 in Pakistan were held on:
mcqs.explanation: Polling day for the 12th General Elections was Thursday, 8 February 2024, across all four provinces, Islamabad Capital Territory and erstwhile FATA merged districts.

mcqs.q: The General Elections of 2024 elected which National Assembly?
mcqs.explanation: The 12th General Elections elected the 16th National Assembly of Pakistan.

mcqs.q: Which political party led the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Provincial Assembly after the 2024 General Elections?
mcqs.explanation: In Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, PTI-backed independents (later the Sunni Ittehad Council, SIC) secured the largest share of seats and formed the provincial government.

mcqs.q: Who became the new Chief Minister of Punjab after the 2024 General Elections?
mcqs.explanation: Maryam Nawaz Sharif of PML-N became Chief Minister of Punjab after the 2024 General Elections, the first woman in Pakistan to hold this office.

mcqs.q: Who became the Chief Minister of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa after the 2024 elections?
mcqs.explanation: Ali Amin Gandapur (PTI-backed) became the Chief Minister of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa after the 2024 General Elections.

mcqs.q: Who became the new Chief Minister of Balochistan after the 2024 elections?
mcqs.explanation: Sarfraz Bugti (PPP) became the Chief Minister of Balochistan, heading a PPP-led provincial coalition government.

mcqs.q: Who is the first woman Chief Minister of Punjab?
mcqs.explanation: Maryam Nawaz Sharif — daughter of Nawaz Sharif and niece of Shehbaz Sharif — is the first woman ever to serve as Chief Minister of Punjab.

mcqs.q: Who became the new Chief Minister of Sindh after the 2024 General Elections?
mcqs.explanation: Syed Murad Ali Shah of PPP was re-elected as Chief Minister of Sindh after the 2024 General Elections.

mcqs.q: The Presidential Election 2024 of Pakistan was held on:
mcqs.explanation: The Presidential Election was held on 9 March 2024. Members of the National Assembly, Senate and the four Provincial Assemblies formed the electoral college.

mcqs.q: Asif Ali Zardari was elected as the ______ President of Pakistan in March 2024.
mcqs.explanation: Asif Ali Zardari became the 14th President of Pakistan. This is his second presidential term — he previously served as 11th President from 2008 to 2013.

mcqs.q: Asif Ali Zardari belongs to which political party?
mcqs.explanation: Asif Ali Zardari is the co-chairman of the Pakistan People's Party (PPP), which he leads jointly with his son Bilawal Bhutto Zardari.

mcqs.q: Which political party led the Punjab Provincial Assembly after the 2024 General Elections?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan Muslim League-Nawaz (PML-N) led the Punjab Provincial Assembly with Maryam Nawaz as Chief Minister.

mcqs.q: Which political party led the Sindh Provincial Assembly after the 2024 General Elections?
mcqs.explanation: The Pakistan People's Party (PPP) again led the Sindh Provincial Assembly, with Murad Ali Shah re-elected as Chief Minister.

mcqs.q: Which party topped the seat tally in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Provincial Assembly after Election 2024?
mcqs.explanation: PTI-backed independents formed the largest bloc in the KP Provincial Assembly and subsequently ran the provincial government under Ali Amin Gandapur.

mcqs.q: The newly elected President of Pakistan in 2024 belongs to the:
mcqs.explanation: President Asif Ali Zardari belongs to the Pakistan People's Party (PPP).

mcqs.q: Who succeeded Dr Arif Alvi as President of Pakistan?
mcqs.explanation: Asif Ali Zardari succeeded Dr Arif Alvi to become the 14th President of Pakistan on 9 March 2024.

mcqs.q: Senate Elections 2024 of Pakistan were held on:
mcqs.explanation: Senate elections were held on 2–3 April 2024, with the new Chairman (Gilani) and Deputy Chairman sworn in thereafter.

mcqs.q: Who was elected as the Speaker of the 16th National Assembly of Pakistan?
mcqs.explanation: Sardar Ayaz Sadiq (PML-N) was elected as Speaker of the 16th National Assembly of Pakistan.

mcqs.q: Who became the Prime Minister of Pakistan after the General Elections of 2024?
mcqs.explanation: Shehbaz Sharif (PML-N) became the Prime Minister of Pakistan for the second time on 3 March 2024, heading a PML-N+PPP-led coalition.

mcqs.q: Which General Elections in Pakistan's history were widely described as the 'most rigged' by opposition parties?
mcqs.explanation: The 12th General Elections of 2024 were widely described as the 'most rigged' in Pakistan's history by opposition parties — especially by PTI and allied independents.

mcqs.q: The 1st General Elections of Pakistan in 1970 were held in:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's first general elections were held in December 1970, with the Awami League in East Pakistan and PPP in West Pakistan emerging as the largest parties.

mcqs.q: The Chief Election Commissioner of Pakistan during the 2024 General Elections was:
mcqs.explanation: Sikandar Sultan Raja served as the Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) of Pakistan during the 2024 General Elections.

mcqs.q: The caretaker Prime Minister of Pakistan before the 2024 General Elections was:
mcqs.explanation: Anwaar-ul-Haq Kakar served as the caretaker Prime Minister of Pakistan from August 2023 until Shehbaz Sharif took oath as PM on 4 March 2024.

mcqs.q: The 2024 General Elections were originally expected in late 2023 but delayed mainly because of:
mcqs.explanation: The polls were delayed from late 2023 to February 2024 primarily to allow the Election Commission to complete the delimitation of constituencies based on the 2023 census.

mcqs.q: Shortly before the 2024 elections, PTI lost its election symbol. The symbol was the:
mcqs.explanation: PTI lost its iconic electoral symbol — the Bat — in late 2023, forcing most PTI-backed candidates to contest the 2024 polls as independents.

mcqs.q: After the 2024 elections, most PTI-backed independents joined which parliamentary party in the National Assembly?
mcqs.explanation: Most PTI-backed independents joined the Sunni Ittehad Council (SIC) to form the largest opposition bloc in the National Assembly.

mcqs.q: Shehbaz Sharif was elected Prime Minister of Pakistan on:
mcqs.explanation: Shehbaz Sharif was elected Prime Minister on 3 March 2024 and took oath on 4 March 2024.

mcqs.q: Who was the PTI-backed opposition candidate for Prime Minister in March 2024?
mcqs.explanation: Omar Ayub Khan was the joint opposition / PTI-backed candidate for Prime Minister, losing to Shehbaz Sharif.

mcqs.q: Shehbaz Sharif became Prime Minister of Pakistan for the:
mcqs.explanation: Shehbaz Sharif became PM for the second time in March 2024; his first term was April 2022 to August 2023.

mcqs.q: Who was elected as Deputy Speaker of the 16th National Assembly?
mcqs.explanation: Syed Ghulam Mustafa Shah (PPP) was elected Deputy Speaker of the 16th National Assembly.

mcqs.q: Who became Leader of the Opposition in the 16th National Assembly?
mcqs.explanation: Omar Ayub Khan was officially recognised as the Leader of the Opposition in the 16th National Assembly of Pakistan.

mcqs.q: Nawaz Sharif contested and won his 2024 National Assembly seat from:
mcqs.explanation: Nawaz Sharif won his seat in the 16th National Assembly from NA-130 Lahore.

mcqs.q: Who was elected Chairman of the Senate of Pakistan in April 2024?
mcqs.explanation: Syed Yousaf Raza Gilani (PPP) was elected as Chairman of the Senate of Pakistan in April 2024.

mcqs.q: Before becoming Chairman Senate in 2024, Syed Yousaf Raza Gilani had previously served as:
mcqs.explanation: Syed Yousaf Raza Gilani served as Prime Minister of Pakistan from 2008 to 2012 before being elected Chairman Senate in 2024.

mcqs.q: Maryam Nawaz Sharif belongs to which political party?
mcqs.explanation: Maryam Nawaz Sharif is a senior leader of Pakistan Muslim League-Nawaz (PML-N) and the daughter of former PM Nawaz Sharif.

mcqs.q: Ali Amin Gandapur, CM of KP after the 2024 elections, is associated with which political party?
mcqs.explanation: Ali Amin Gandapur is associated with Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) and, like other PTI-backed leaders, used the SIC platform in Parliament.

mcqs.q: Syed Murad Ali Shah belongs to which political party?
mcqs.explanation: Syed Murad Ali Shah is a senior leader of the Pakistan People's Party (PPP) and has served as Chief Minister of Sindh multiple times.

mcqs.q: Sarfraz Bugti, CM of Balochistan after the 2024 elections, belongs to:
mcqs.explanation: Sarfraz Bugti joined the Pakistan People's Party (PPP) before the 2024 elections and became Chief Minister of Balochistan under a PPP-led provincial coalition.

mcqs.q: Who was the main opponent of Asif Ali Zardari in the 2024 Presidential Election?
mcqs.explanation: Mahmood Khan Achakzai, head of Pakhtunkhwa Milli Awami Party (PkMAP), was the joint opposition candidate against Asif Ali Zardari in the 2024 Presidential Election.

mcqs.q: Asif Ali Zardari's first presidential term (as 11th President) was during:
mcqs.explanation: Asif Ali Zardari served as the 11th President of Pakistan from 2008 to 2013 — becoming the first person to be elected President of Pakistan twice in 2024.

mcqs.q: The electoral college that elects the President of Pakistan consists of members of the:
options: National Assembly, Senate and all four Provincial Assemblies
mcqs.explanation: Under Article 41 of the 1973 Constitution, the President is elected by an electoral college comprising both houses of Parliament (NA and Senate) and all four Provincial Assemblies.

mcqs.q: Asif Ali Zardari's co-leader of the PPP is his son:
mcqs.explanation: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari is the Chairman of the PPP and co-leads the party with his father, Asif Ali Zardari.

mcqs.q: Before Maryam Nawaz, Punjab had never had a woman Chief Minister. Nationally, the first woman Chief Minister of any Pakistani province was:
options: None — Maryam Nawaz is the first ever
mcqs.explanation: Maryam Nawaz Sharif is the first woman Chief Minister of any province in Pakistan's history — no Pakistani province had elected a woman CM before her.

mcqs.q: The 11th General Elections of Pakistan (2018) were won by which party?
mcqs.explanation: The 11th General Elections (July 2018) were won by Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI), with Imran Khan becoming Prime Minister.
